records.chapter: The Reward of the Prayer Known Among People as the Prayer of Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {s.a}, Also Called the Prayer of the Devout (awabeen)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1556 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"Whoever performs ablution and completes it thoroughly, then begins the prayer and prays four units (rak'ahs), separating each two with a taslim (salutation), and recites in each rak'ah the Opening of the Book (Surah al-Fatiha) once and 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) fifty times, he will finish his prayer with nothing between him and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, except that his sins will be forgiven."
records.arabicText: 1556 - رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَنْ تَوَضَّأَ فَأَسْبَغَ اَلْوُضُوءَ وَ اِفْتَتَحَ اَلصَّلاَةَ فَصَلَّى أَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَهُنَّ بِتَسْلِيمَةٍ يَقْرَأُ فِي كُلِّ رَكْعَةٍ فَاتِحَةَ اَلْكِتَابِ مَرَّةً وَ «قُلْ هُوَ اَللّٰهُ أَحَدٌ» خَمْسِينَ مَرَّةً اِنْفَتَلَ حِينَ يَنْفَتِلُ وَ لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ذَنْبٌ إِلاَّ غَفَرَ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1556 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever performs ablution and completes it thoroughly, then begins the prayer and prays four units (rak'ahs), separating each two with a taslim (salutation), and recites in each rak'ah the Opening of the Book (Surah al-Fatiha) once and 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) fifty times, he will finish his prayer with nothing between him and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious, except that his sins will be forgiven."

records.chapter: The Reward of the Prayer Known Among People as the Prayer of Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {s.a}, Also Called the Prayer of the Devout (awabeen)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1557 - Muhammad ibn Mas'ud al-Ayyashi, may Allah (swt) have mercy on him, narrated in his book from Abdullah ibn Muhammad, from Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn al-Samak, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"Whoever prays four rak'ahs (units of prayer) and recites in each rak'ah 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) fifty times, it is the Prayer of Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {S.A}, and it is the prayer of those who turn to Allah (swt) in repentance (al-Awwabin)."
records.arabicText: 1557 - وَ أَمَّا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مَسْعُودٍ اَلْعَيَّاشِيُّ رَحِمَهُ اَللَّهُ فَقَدْ رَوَى فِي كِتَابِهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ اَلسِّمَاكِ عَنِ اِبْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَنْ صَلَّى أَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ فَقَرَأَ فِي كُلِّ رَكْعَةٍ بِخَمْسِينَ مَرَّةً «قُلْ هُوَ اَللّٰهُ أَحَدٌ » كَانَتْ صَلاَةَ فَاطِمَةَ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ هِيَ صَلاَةُ اَلْأَوَّابِينَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1557 - Muhammad ibn Mas'ud al-Ayyashi, may Allah (swt) have mercy on him, narrated in his book from Abdullah ibn Muhammad, from Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn al-Samak, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever prays four rak'ahs (units of prayer) and recites in each rak'ah 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) fifty times, it is the Prayer of Sayyida Fatimah Al-Zahra {S.A}, and it is the prayer of those who turn to Allah (swt) in repentance (al-Awwabin)."

records.chapter: The Reward of Two rak'ahs with the Recitation of Surah Al-Ikhlas 120 Times
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1558 - In a narration from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Al-Sadiq (as), He said:
"Whoever prays two light (short) units of prayer (Rak'ahs), reciting 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) sixty times in each Rak'ah, will finish the prayer without any sins remaining between him and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Exalted."
records.arabicText: 1558 - فِي رِوَايَةِ اِبْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَنْ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ بِ‍ «قُلْ هُوَ اَللّٰهُ أَحَدٌ»  فِي كُلِّ رَكْعَةٍ سِتِّينَ مَرَّةً اِنْفَتَلَ وَ لَيْسَ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ذَنْبٌ» .
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1558 - In a narration from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Al-Sadiq (as), He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever prays two light (short) units of prayer (Rak'ahs), reciting 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) sixty times in each Rak'ah, will finish the prayer without any sins remaining between him and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Exalted."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: It appears that what is meant by "al-Nawadir" are uncommon reports that do not fall under a specific chapter and are dispersed. The term may also refer to rare or unusual narrations.
records.arabicText: الظاهر أن المراد بالنوادر التي لا يجمعها باب وتكون متفرقة، وقد يطلق على الاخبار الشاذة.

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1559 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Perform supererogatory (extra) prayers during the time of heedlessness, even if it is just two light rak'ahs (units of prayer), for they lead to the abode of honor."
records.arabicText: 1559 - قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «تَنَفَّلُوا فِي سَاعَةِ اَلْغَفْلَةِ وَ لَوْ بِرَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ فَإِنَّهُمَا تُورِثَانِ دَارَ اَلْكَرَامَةِ.

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.1560 - In another narration: "The Abode of Peace."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1561 - Bukayr ibn A'yan narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) never prayed the Duha prayer."
records.arabicText: 1561 - رَوَى بُكَيْرُ بْنُ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «مَا صَلَّى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلضُّحَى قَطُّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1561 - Bukayr ibn A'yan narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (sw) never prayed the Duha prayer."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1562 - Abdul Wahid ibn al-Mukhtar al-Ansari narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
"I asked him about the Duha prayer”
Imam (as) said: ‘The first people to perform it were your people, for they were among the heedless, so they performed it.
The Messenger of Allah (sw) did not perform it.'
He also said: 'Imam Ali (as) passed by a man who was performing it, and Imam Ali (as) said: ‘What is this prayer?’
The man replied: ‘Should I leave it, O’ Commander of the Faithful?’
He (Imam Ali (as)) said: ‘Would I forbid a servant when he prays?’".
records.arabicText: 1562 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلْوَاحِدِ بْنُ اَلْمُخْتَارِ اَلْأَنْصَارِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ صَلاَةِ اَلضُّحَى فَقَالَ «أَوَّلُ مَنْ صَلاَّهَا قَوْمُكَ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا مِنَ اَلْغَافِلِينَ فَيُصَلُّونَهَا وَ لَمْ يُصَلِّهَا رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ » وَقَالَ «إِنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَرَّ عَلَى رَجُلٍ وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّيهَا فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «مَا هَذِهِ اَلصَّلاَةُ» فَقَالَ أَدَعُهَا يَا أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «أَكُونُ أَنْهَى «عَبْداً إِذٰا صَلّٰى.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1562 - Abdul Wahid ibn al-Mukhtar al-Ansari narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about the Duha prayer”
Imam (as) said: ‘The first people to perform it were your people, for they were among the heedless, so they performed it.
The Messenger of Allah (sw) did not perform it.'
He also said: 'Imam Ali (as) passed by a man who was performing it, and Imam Ali (as) said: ‘What is this prayer?’
The man replied: ‘Should I leave it, O’ Commander of the Faithful?’
He (Imam Ali (as)) said: ‘Would I forbid a servant when he prays?’".

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1563 – Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) never prayed the Duha prayer."
So I (Zurarah) said to him, "Did you not inform me that He (sw) used to pray four rak'ahs in the forenoon?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, but he used to count it among the eight rak'ahs that are prayed after Dhuhr."
records.arabicText: 1563 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «مَا صَلَّى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلضُّحَى قَطُّ» قَالَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ أَ لَمْ تُخْبِرْنِي أَنَّهُ كَانَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُصَلِّي فِي صَدْرِ اَلنَّهَارِ أَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ قَالَ «بَلَى إِنَّهُ كَانَ يَجْعَلُهَا مِنَ اَلثَّمَانِ اَلَّتِي بَعْدَ اَلظُّهْرِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1563 – Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) never prayed the Duha prayer."
So I (Zurarah) said to him, "Did you not inform me that He (sw) used to pray four rak'ahs in the forenoon?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, but he used to count it among the eight rak'ahs that are prayed after Dhuhr."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1564 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the prayer during the month of Ramadan.
Imam (as) said: "Thirteen rak'ahs, including the Witr prayer and two rak'ahs before the Fajr prayer. This is how the Messenger of Allah (sw) used to pray, and if there was any greater virtue in adding more, the Messenger of Allah (sw) would have been the most diligent and deserving of it."
records.arabicText: 1564 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ ، أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ فَقَالَ «ثَلاَثَ عَشْرَةَ رَكْعَةً مِنْهَا اَلْوَتْرُ وَ رَكْعَتَانِ قَبْلَ صَلاَةِ اَلْفَجْرِ كَذَلِكَ كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يُصَلِّي وَ لَوْ كَانَ فَضْلاً كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَعْمَلَ بِهِ وَ أَحَقَّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1564 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the prayer during the month of Ramadan.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Thirteen rak'ahs, including the Witr prayer and two rak'ahs before the Fajr prayer. This is how the Messenger of Allah (sw) used to pray, and if there was any greater virtue in adding more, the Messenger of Allah (sw) would have been the most diligent and deserving of it."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1565 - Uqbah ibn Khalid asked Him (Imam Abu Abdullah (as)) about a man who was praying and another man called out to him, causing him to become distracted and respond to the person's request. What should he do?
Imam (as) said: "He should continue with his prayer."
records.arabicText: 1565 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عُقْبَةُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ: عَنْ رَجُلٍ دَعَاهُ رَجُلٌ وَ هُوَ يُصَلِّي فَسَهَا فَأَجَابَهُ بِحَاجَتِهِ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ «يَمْضِي عَلَى صَلاَتِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1565 - Uqbah ibn Khalid asked Him (Imam Abu Abdullah (as)) about a man who was praying and another man called out to him, causing him to become distracted and respond to the person's request. What should he do?
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He should continue with his prayer."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1566 - Imran Al-Halabi narrated from Imam (as) that He said:
"It is recommended to lighten (shorten) the prayer because of forgetfulness."
records.arabicText: 1566 - وَ رَوَى عِمْرَانُ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْهُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «يَنْبَغِي تَخْفِيفُ اَلصَّلاَةِ مِنْ أَجْلِ اَلسَّهْوِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1566 - Imran Al-Halabi narrated from Imam (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is recommended to lighten (shorten) the prayer because of forgetfulness."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1567 - Sama’a bin Mehran narrated from Imam (as) that He said:
"The charity given by a boy, his emancipation (freeing a slave), and leading people in prayer are permissible if he is ten years old."
records.arabicText: 1567 - وَ رَوَى سَمَاعَةُ بْنُ مِهْرَانَ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «يَجُوزُ صَدَقَةُ اَلْغُلاَمِ وَ عِتْقُهُ وَ يَؤُمُّ اَلنَّاسَ إِذَا كَانَ لَهُ عَشْرُ سِنِينَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1567 - Sama’a bin Mehran narrated from Imam (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The charity given by a boy, his emancipation (freeing a slave), and leading people in prayer are permissible if he is ten years old."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1568 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If you pray with them (in congregation), you will be forgiven according to the number of those who oppose you."
records.arabicText: 1568 - وقال الصادق عليه السلام: " إذا صليت معهم غفر لك بعدد من خالفك.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1568 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you pray with them (in congregation), you will be forgiven according to the number of those who oppose you."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1569 - It is narrated from Him (Imam Al-Sadiq (as)) through Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah that Imam (as) said:
"If you pray, then pray in your sandals (shoes) if they are clean, for that is from the Sunnah."
records.arabicText: 1569 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ فَصَلِّ فِي نَعْلَيْكَ إِذَا كَانَتْ طَاهِرَةً فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ مِنَ اَلسُّنَّةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1569 - It is narrated from Him (Imam Al-Sadiq (as)) through Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdillah that Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you pray, then pray in your sandals (shoes) if they are clean, for that is from the Sunnah."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1570 - It is narrated by Al-Halabi from Him (Imam Al-Sadiq (as)) that He said:
"If you pray any of the prayers during travel outside of its (regular) time, it will not harm you."
records.arabicText: 1570 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ شَيْئاً مِنَ اَلصَّلَوَاتِ فِي غَيْرِ وَقْتِهَا فَلاَ يَضُرُّكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1570 - It is narrated by Al-Halabi from Him (Imam Al-Sadiq (as)) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you pray any of the prayers during travel outside of its (regular) time, it will not harm you."

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1571 - It is narrated from Aidh al-Ahmasi that He said:
"I entered upon Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) while I intended to ask him about prayer. Without me asking, he began (by saying):
'If you meet Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, with the five obligatory prayers, He will not ask you about anything beyond that.'"
records.arabicText: 1571 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَائِذٍ اَلْأَحْمَسِيِّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَسْأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فَابْتَدَأَنِي مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ أَسْأَلَهُ فَقَالَ «إِذَا لَقِيتَ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِالصَّلَوَاتِ اَلْخَمْسِ اَلْمَفْرُوضَاتِ لَمْ يَسْأَلْكَ عَمَّا سِوَى ذَلِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.1571 - It is narrated from Aidh al-Ahmasi that He said:
"I entered upon Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) while I intended to ask him about prayer. Without me asking, he began (by saying):
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'If you meet Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, with the five obligatory prayers, He will not ask you about anything beyond that.'"

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1572 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"A believer remains in a state of spiritual reward (mu'aqqib) as long as they are in a state of ritual purity (wudhu)”.
records.arabicText: 1572 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلْمُؤْمِنُ مُعَقِّبٌ مَا دَامَ عَلَى وُضُوءٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1572 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A believer remains in a state of spiritual reward (mu'aqqib) as long as they are in a state of ritual purity (wudhu)”.

records.chapter: The Reward of Voluntary Prayers During the Time of Heedlessness
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.1573 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
I asked him about a person who has so many missed (Qadha) prayers that he cannot count them, what should he do?
Imam (as) said: "Let him pray until he no longer knows how many he has prayed due to their abundance, and he would have compensated for the amount he is aware of."
I then asked: "What if he cannot complete all the Qadha prayers?"
Imam (as) said: "If his preoccupation is due to seeking a necessary livelihood or helping a fellow believer in need, there is no obligation on him. But if his preoccupation is for gathering worldly gain and being distracted from prayer, then he must make up for them; otherwise, he will meet Allah (swt) while being negligent, belittling, and neglecting the sanctity of Messenger of Allah (sw)."
I asked: "What if he still cannot make up for them? Is charity sufficient?"
The Imam remained silent for a while and then said: "Let him give charity."
I asked: "How much charity?"
Imam (as) said: "As much as he can afford, with the minimum being a 'mud' (a specific measure) of food for each missed prayer."
I asked: "How many prayers require a 'mud' of charity?"
Imam (as) said: "A 'mud' for every two units (rak'ahs) of the night prayer and a 'mud' for every two units of the day prayer."
I said: "What if he cannot do this?"
Imam (as) said: "Then one 'mud' for every four units of the day prayer."
I said: "What if he cannot manage even this?"
Imam (as) said: "Then one 'mud' for the entire night prayer and one 'mud' for the entire day prayer. However, prayer is superior, and prayer is superior, and prayer is superior."
records.arabicText: 1573 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ أَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ صَلاَةِ اَلنَّوَافِلِ مَا لاَ يَدْرِي مَا هُوَ مِنْ كَثْرَتِهَا كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ «فَلْيُصَلِّ حَتَّى لاَ يَدْرِيَ كَمْ صَلَّى مِنْ كَثْرَتِهَا فَيَكُونَ قَدْ قَضَى بِقَدْرِ مَا عَلِمَهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، » ثُمَّ قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَقْدِرُ عَلَى اَلْقَضَاءِ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ شُغُلُهُ فِي طَلَبِ مَعِيشَةٍ لاَ بُدَّ مِنْهَا أَوْ حَاجَةٍ لِأَخٍ مُؤْمِنٍ فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ شُغُلُهُ لِجَمْعِ اَلدُّنْيَا وَ اَلتَّشَاغُلِ بِهَا عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فَعَلَيْهِ اَلْقَضَاءُ وَ إِلاَّ لَقِيَ اَللَّهَ وَ هُوَ مُسْتَخِفٌّ مُتَهَاوِنٌ مُضَيِّعٌ لِحُرْمَةِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ » قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَقْدِرُ عَلَى اَلْقَضَاءِ فَهَلْ يُجْزِي أَنْ يَتَصَدَّقَ فَسَكَتَ مَلِيّاً ثُمَّ قَالَ «فَلْيَتَصَدَّقْ بِصَدَقَةٍ» قُلْتُ فَمَا يَتَصَدَّقُ قَالَ «بِقَدْرِ طَوْلِهِ وَ أَدْنَى ذَلِكَ مُدٌّ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ مَكَانَ كُلِّ صَلاَةٍ» قُلْتُ وَ كَمِ اَلصَّلاَةُ اَلَّتِي يَجِبُ فِيهَا مُدٌّ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ قَالَ «لِكُلِّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ مِنْ صَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ مُدٌّ وَ لِكُلِّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ مِنْ صَلاَةِ اَلنَّهَارِ مُدٌّ» فَقُلْتُ لاَ يَقْدِرُ فَقَالَ «مُدٌّ إِذاً لِكُلِّ أَرْبَعِ رَكَعَاتٍ مِنْ صَلاَةِ اَلنَّهَارِ» قُلْتُ لاَ يَقْدِرُ قَالَ «فَمُدٌّ إِذاً لِصَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ وَ مُدٌّ لِصَلاَةِ اَلنَّهَارِ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ أَفْضَلُ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ أَفْضَلُ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ أَفْضَلُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.1573 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him about a person who has so many missed (Qadha) prayers that he cannot count them, what should he do?
Imam (as) said: "Let him pray until he no longer knows how many he has prayed due to their abundance, and he would have compensated for the amount he is aware of."
I then asked: "What if he cannot complete all the Qadha prayers?"
Imam (as) said: "If his preoccupation is due to seeking a necessary livelihood or helping a fellow believer in need, there is no obligation on him. But if his preoccupation is for gathering worldly gain and being distracted from prayer, then he must make up for them; otherwise, he will meet Allah (swt) while being negligent, belittling, and neglecting the sanctity of Messenger of Allah (sw)."
I asked: "What if he still cannot make up for them? Is charity sufficient?"
The Imam remained silent for a while and then said: "Let him give charity."
I asked: "How much charity?"
Imam (as) said: "As much as he can afford, with the minimum being a 'mud' (a specific measure) of food for each missed prayer."
I asked: "How many prayers require a 'mud' of charity?"
Imam (as) said: "A 'mud' for every two units (rak'ahs) of the night prayer and a 'mud' for every two units of the day prayer."
I said: "What if he cannot do this?"
Imam (as) said: "Then one 'mud' for every four units of the day prayer."
I said: "What if he cannot manage even this?"
Imam (as) said: "Then one 'mud' for the entire night prayer and one 'mud' for the entire day prayer. However, prayer is superior, and prayer is superior, and prayer is superior."
